label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out this ethical dilemma about my best friend who plagiarized their assignment. The question is whether to report them or stay silent. Let me start by breaking down the different aspects here. First, the main people involved are me, my friend, the professor, and maybe the class in general. The ethical principles in play are honesty, integrity, friendship, loyalty, responsibility to the institution, and maybe fairness or justice. If I report my friend, they might get in trouble academically. The professor could give them a failing grade, maybe even expel them. That would hurt their academic career and possibly their future. On the other hand, if I stay silent, they get to keep their grade, which is unfair to other students who worked hard. Also, it's allowing them to cheat, which is against the school's policies. Honesty and integrity are important to me as a person. If I don't report it, am I being complicit in their dishonesty? That could mean I'm not living up to my own values. But then again, my friendship with them is a big part of my life. Reporting them could damage that relationship, maybe even end it. What if they had a good reason for plagiarizing? Like maybe they were really overwhelmed, stressed, or had personal issues. But the scenario says they just wanted to avoid failing. So maybe their reasoning isn't as compelling. Still, they might have had a moment of weakness. I should consider the consequences of both actions. Reporting them could lead to them facing the consequences and maybe learning from it. They might not do it again. But if I don't report them, they might think it's okay to continue cheating. That could set a bad precedent. Also, the institution's policies are there to maintain academic standards. If everyone cheated and got away with it, the system would break down. By reporting it, I'm upholding those standards. But on a personal level, it's a hard choice because of the friendship. Are there any other ethical principles to consider? Maybe autonomy—my friend has the right to make their own choices, even if they're bad ones. But does that mean I should stay silent? Or is it my responsibility to intervene when someone is violating ethical standards? What if I talk to my friend first before reporting? I could tell them what I found out and give them a chance to confess and maybe get a lighter punishment. That way, I'm being honest with them and giving them an option. But if they don't want to confess, then maybe I have to report it. Also, would
